notes: NOAA CPC operational bands. ≤0.5 neutral; 0.5–1.0 weak El Niño (watch); 1.0–1.5 moderate (alert); >1.5 strong/very strong (emergency-class). Symmetric La Niña handled by absolute value upstream.
BoM SOI. Sustained negative values indicate El Niño. >-7 neutral; -7 to -10 El Niño watch; -10 to -15 alert; <-15 strong event.
Niño 3.4 monthly SST anomaly. Aligned with ONI; included separately so weekly SST can escalate before the 3-month ONI does.
PNG highland drought thresholds. -20% near-normal; -40% drought watch; -60% severe drought (1997 and 2015 events both >-60%). Inverted: more negative = worse.
Heat-stress proxy. Combined with rainfall deficit it drives crop and health risk in the highlands.
NASA SMAP-derived percentile. Inverted: lower percentile = drier = worse. <10th percentile = exceptional drought.
MODIS NDVI anomaly. Vegetation stress lags rainfall by ~4–8 weeks; an early canary for food-security collapse.

unit: count of M4.5+ events in PNG (30-day window)
notes: USGS FDSNWS earthquake tempo for the PNG bbox. PNG sits on the Ring of Fire; a typical month sees ~10–25 M4.5+ events. Elevated tempo (>25) flags a seismically active period; a major shock or swarm pushes >45. Non-inverted: higher count = worse.
